title: melaleuca curtifolia
intro: Melaleuca curtifolia, also known as the Lemon Bottlebrush, is a shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the myrtle family (Myrtaceae). The plant's leaves and young shoots contain essential oils, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Melaleuca curtifolia make it useful for treating various health issues, including cold sores, wounds, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tea, salves, and tinctures, which may be used to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
